items.title: Apple Upgraded to Buy by HSBC on Agentic AI, Hardware Pipeline
items.description: Apple Inc. was upgraded to buy at HSBC Holdings Plc on Friday, in the latest reflection of how Wall Street is increasingly positive on the iPhone maker’s position in a market where many trades related

items.title: Oil Market Can’t Live Without Hormuz Barrels for Long
items.description: Flows through the critical waterway haven’t fully stopped, but signs of stress in the market are mounting.

items.title: Innolight Gets Approval for Up to $8 Billion Hong Kong Listing
items.description: Zhongji Innolight Co. received approval from China’s securities regulator and the Hong Kong stock exchange for what is likely to be one of the city’s biggest listings in years.

items.title: Neglected Quant Safety Trade Makes a Comeback as AI Risks Rise
items.description: As worries build around the artificial intelligence trade, investors are starting to seek shelter in a more pedestrian corner of the stock market: companies with sturdy finances.

items.title: Reliance Founders Lift Stake by Most Since 2019 as Stock Slumps
items.description: Reliance Industries Ltd. founders, led by billionaire Mukesh Ambani, raised stake in the Indian oil-to-telecom conglomerate by the most in nearly seven years following the stock’s prolonged slump in t

items.title: Lula’s Panda-Bond Push Tests China’s Appeal for Brazil’s Firms
items.description: Brazil has big ambitions for its plan to enter China’s onshore bond market — if only corporate Brazil follows along.

items.title: Hyperscalers Are Dragging Down Bond Gauges Across Global Markets
items.description: The bonds sold by hyperscalers to fuel their artificial intelligence ambitions have become a drag on investor portfolios from London to Tokyo.

items.title: IBM’s Warning Is a ‘Hammer’ Slamming Down on Tech’s AI Outsiders
items.description: International Business Machines Corp.’s warning this week that its sales are falling well short of expectations highlights a growing divide between artificial intelligence winners and everyone else in

items.title: Circle Gets Street-Low Target as Stablecoin Competition Heats Up
items.description: Circle Internet Group Inc. shares have lost more than three-quarters of their value from the highs seen just after their initial public offering last year and as more stablecoins come to market, one a

items.title: Mining Equipment Makers Sandvik, Epiroc Fall as Orders Slow
items.description: Shares in mining equipment manufacturers Sandvik AB and Epiroc AB fell as a slide in precious metal prices since the start of the year weiged on orders.

items.title: Chip Stocks Lead Selloff as Investors Question AI Assumptions
items.description: Tech stocks are selling off and there’s no sign of any letup. A new AI model from a Chinese startup is one contributing factor.

items.title: Amundi Says Oil, Gas Crucial to EU’s Clean-Energy Transition
items.description: Amundi SA wants the European Union to free asset managers to add oil and gas exposures to a new fund category intended to support the transition to a lower-carbon economy.

items.title: UBS AM’s Zhao Sours on Treasuries as US Growth Beats Europe
items.description: UBS Asset Management’s Kevin Zhao is planning to short Treasuries in a bet that the robust US economy will erode the haven appeal of owning government debt.

items.title: US Stock Futures Drop as New AI Model Draws DeepSeek Comparisons
items.description: US stock-index futures dropped on Friday as the selloff in semiconductor names gathered steam following the reveal of an artificial-intelligence model from a Chinese startup.

items.title: Coronation Trims TSMC, SK Hynix on AI Hype, Boosts India Stocks
items.description: Coronation Asset Management Ltd., which oversees $47 billion, has trimmed its exposure to chipmakers and increased its allocation to India, as expectations for artificial intelligence stocks have rise

items.title: Wheat Fluctuates as Traders Weigh Black Sea Risks, US Exports
items.description: Wheat fluctuated after hitting a two-year high in the previous session as traders weighed Black Sea supply risks against weak US export data.

items.title: Copper Follows Stock Markets Lower With AI Selloff Deepening
items.description: Copper and other base metals retreated on Friday, tracking losses in equity markets as investor worries about the valuation of artificial-intelligence companies grow.

items.title: Gold Heads for Second Weekly Drop as War Raises Fed Hike Fears
items.description: Gold was on track for the biggest weekly loss since early June as renewed hostilities in the Middle East raised concerns that higher interest rates will be needed to contain war-driven inflation.

items.title: Nasdaq Futures Tumble 1.9% as Chip Rout Deepens: Markets Wrap
items.description: Nasdaq 100 futures tumbled 1.9% as the global selloff in chipmakers gathered pace, with traders rushing to exit positions in stocks that had fueled this year’s rally but whose valuations grew increasi

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 17
items.description: Oil was heading for its biggest weekly advance since April on mounting signs that the Middle East conflict is escalating, threatening flows through the Strait of Hormuz.
